headlines.title: Chevron CEO Warns of Emerging Physical Shortages in Crude Oil
headlines.summary: Signs of physical shortages in crude oil are emerging, the chief executive of Chevron warned this week, noting that this will first hit Asian economic growth. “We will start to see physical shortages,” Mike Wirth said at an event, as quoted by Reuters. “Demand needs to move to me

headlines.title: Big Oil Resists Push To Prioritize Output Growth
headlines.summary: It is profit season again, and Big Oil is raking it in, both in Europe and in the U.S., as soaring oil and gas prices boost earnings. What they are not boosting, however, is production. Big Oil is signaling it has no plans to rearrange its priorities. Higher oil prices drove the

headlines.title: ADNOC Accelerates $55 Billion Investment after UAE’s OPEC Exit
headlines.summary: The Abu Dhabi national oil company, ADNOC, is accelerating investment in growth and production after the United Arab Emirates (UAE) left OPEC on May 1. headlines.title: Volume Down Revenue Up Iran’s Oil Trade Defies the War
headlines.summary: The 2026 US-Israeli war against Iran delivered one of the sharpest ironies in modern energy geopolitics: while Iranian infrastructure was taking a pounding, it managed to somewhat absorb the relentless airstrikes to its culture, its way of life, and its main target—the economy. headlines.title: Why the U.S. Is Pouring Billions Into Energy Projects in the Balkans
headlines.summary: Since US President Donald Trump took office last year, Washington and Brussels have clashed over trade, support for Ukraine, and military spending. headlines.title: Oil Surges to $114 Following Iranian Strikes on UAE Port
headlines.summary: A fragile four-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran appears to be on the brink of collapse following a violent exchange of fire in the Persian Gulf on Monday. headlines.title: UK Faces Summer Flight Disruptions as Jet Fuel Risks Mount
headlines.summary: Ministers are set to warn the British public that flight cancellations will hit summer holiday plans as new research suggested that the UK is more exposed to jet fuel shortages than other European countries. headlines.title: Equinor Signs $1.8 Billion in Drilling Deals to Keep Oil and Gas Output High
headlines.summary: Equinor has extended $1.8 billion worth of drilling and well services supplier contracts as the Norwegian energy major looks to keep high oil and gas production offshore Norway and deliver stable energy volumes to Europe.
